decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.29°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution and reduce thermal resistance. I will apply a conservative 1.5x increase (from 126.0 to 189.0 W/(m*K)), which is within the allowed [0.5x, 2.0x] step ratio and well below the upper bound of 500.0.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, lowering the local hot spot temperature on the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below the 85.0°C constraint.
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader layer.
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
Further iterations may require geometry changes (spreader width/height) if material tuning alone is insufficient.
